headline: Refugee work permits pilot covers only 450 individuals
context: A limited pilot program granting work permits to refugees, launched in 2024, covered only 450 of 180,000 registered refugees.

cards.big: Work permit pilot reaches only 450 of 180,000 registered refugees in Malaysia
cards.sub: 0.25% coverage rate renders the pilot symbolically significant but practically irrelevant

cards.big: Immigration Act 1959 does not recognise refugee status or right to work
cards.sub: Pilot operates through administrative discretion without legislative backing

cards.big: ILO estimates 120,000 refugees work informally in Malaysia facing exploitation
cards.sub: Without legal work rights, refugees accept below-minimum wages and unsafe conditions

cards.big: Refugees could contribute estimated RM3.2 billion annually if legally employed
cards.sub: Current informal economy participation yields no tax revenue for Malaysia

cards.big: 450 permits for 180,000 people is not a pilot, it is a token
UNHCR proposed expanding pilot to 10,000 permits in 2025 submission to government
